channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'Everybody talks about what's the next AI device... Glasses, obviously is one of them' — Samsung exec teases details about its forthcoming XR glasses, and when they might arrive
item.description: It looks increasingly likely that we'll see the Samsung XR glasses before the end of the current year.

item.title: I haven’t sat in a plusher gaming chair than this one – but this affordable GTPlayer model has one drawback
item.description: The GTPlayer LR002 entices with an affordable price and a plush frame – but is it as comfortable as it looks?

item.title: I've hunted out the best Galaxy S26 cases to keep your swanky new phone protected
item.description: Samsung Galaxy S26 is about to start shipping, so it's time to get your case sorted now.

item.title: Roblox introduces real-time AI-powered chat rephraser for inappropriate language
item.description: Roblox has launched a feature powered by AI that can rephrase inappropriate language in real time. The online game has been using AI filters to block out any language that goes against its policy for a while now, but it has been replacing censored chats with a series of hash signs (####). Roblox admits that encountering too many hashmarks can be disruptive and make conversations hard to follow. This new feature will instead replace words and phrases with what the AI deems as more appropriate substitutes. Rajiv Bhatia, Roblox’s Chief Safety Office, said the game is starting with profanity. For ...

item.title: Et si nous cherchions mal ? Une étude du Seti suggère que les signaux aliens pourraient nous échapper
item.description: Les signaux radio envoyés par des extraterrestres pourraient être réels, mais ne pas nous parvenir. En cause : les étoiles qui affaibliraient les émissions, jusqu'à les rendre inaudibles pour nous.

item.title: Vizio accounts are becoming Walmart accounts
item.description: Walmart bought Vizio in 2024, and now it's taking the next steps to formally fold the TV maker into itself by phasing out Vizio accounts. Now, when you purchase a new Vizio TV, you'll be asked to sign in or create a Walmart account. Customers will have the option to merge their Vizio account, if […]

item.title: Pixar Is Developing ‘Monsters Inc. 3’, a Ghost Story and Its First Musical
item.description: New blood and some returning faves are all being cooked up over at Pixar and hitting theaters in the near future.

item.title: Polymarket and Kalshi’s CEOs Hate Each Other
These States Are Joining in the Push to Ban Surveillance Pricing
item.description: At least a dozen states are considering legislation.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Sunday, March 8 (game #1001)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Sunday, March 8 (game #735)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Leading by example: Embracing tools internally before shipping them externally
item.description: Using your own tools builds trust, improves quality, accelerates innovation, and protects customers from failure.

item.title: Cet écran tire le meilleur de votre vidéoprojecteur : j’ai testé le Nothing Projector ALR Longue focale
item.description: L’écran Nothing Projector ALR Longue focale promet de transformer un simple vidéoprojecteur en véritable installation home cinéma, même dans une pièce de vie. Pensé pour les projecteurs longue focale et standard, en voici nos impressions.
